evidence: Docstring: n can be written as x + y such that both x + ny and x^2 + ny^2 are prime.
verify_note: decl OeisA232174.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live, label matches; primality of x+ny and x^2+ny^2 is the core condition

evidence: Docstring: every prime p has a primitive root 0 < g < p of the form k^2 + 1.
verify_note: decl OeisA239957.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; primitive root (orderOf = p-1) is the very object of the conjecture

evidence: The conjecture quantifies over every prime p and its primitive roots.
verify_note: decl OeisA239957.conjecture exists; QID live; statement quantifies over all primes p — load-bearing

evidence: Reference is Z.-W. Sun, 'Refining Lagrange's four-square theorem'; the conjecture adds a square condition to the four-square representation.
verify_note: decl OeisA280831.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; conjecture is an explicit refinement of Lagrange's four-square theorem (Sun ref)

evidence: Docstring: n = x^2 + y^2 + z^2 + w^2 with x^4 + 1680 y^3 z a square.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; n as sum of four squares plus an IsSquare condition — squares are load-bearing

evidence: Reference is Z.-W. Sun, 'Refining Lagrange's four-square theorem'; the conjecture restricts the four-square representation so x and x + 24y are squares.
verify_note: decl OeisA281976.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; explicit refinement of Lagrange's four-square theorem (Sun ref)

evidence: Docstring: n = x^2 + y^2 + z^2 + w^2 with both x and x + 24y squares.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; four squares with x and x+24y required to be squares — load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: sum of a triangular number x(x+1)/2, a generalized pentagonal number, and a generalized heptagonal number.
verify_note: decl OeisA287616.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; triangular number x(x+1)/2 is one of the three summands

evidence: Docstring names the generalized pentagonal number y(3y+1)/2 as one of the three summands.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; generalized pentagonal number y(3y+1)/2 named as summand

evidence: Docstring names the generalized heptagonal number z(5z+1)/2 as one of the three summands.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; generalized heptagonal number z(5z+1)/2 named as summand

evidence: Docstring: any integer n > 1 is a sum of two squares, a power of 3, and a power of 5.
verify_note: decl OeisA303656.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; sum of two squares is half the statement

evidence: The 2-4-6-8 Conjecture writes every positive integer as a sum of four binomial coefficients C(w+2,2)+C(x+3,4)+C(y+5,6)+C(z+7,8).
verify_note: decl OeisA306477.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; the statement is a sum of four binomial coefficients

evidence: References are Sun's 'Refining Lagrange's four-square theorem' and 'Restricted sums of four squares'; the conjecture restricts two of the four squares to (2^a 3^b)^2 and (2^c 5^d)^2.
verify_note: decl OeisA308734.conjecture exists @[category research open]; QID live; explicit refinement of Lagrange's four-square theorem (Sun refs)

evidence: Docstring: n = (2^a*3^b)^2 + (2^c*5^d)^2 + x^2 + y^2, a restricted sum of four squares.
verify_note: decl exists; QID live; restricted sum of four squares — load-bearing

evidence: Docstring: for every n > 1 there exists k < n such that nk + 1 is a prime (A34693 is the smallest such k).
verify_note: decl OeisA34693.exists_k exists @[category research open]; QID live; primality of nk+1 is the whole statement
